item.title: Best Fire Deck in Pokémon TCG Pocket: All Cards & Strategy Guide
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon TCG Pocket has a wide array of cards to use in deck building, but some remain more powerful than others, and Fire decks are a popular approach when opting for the best decks in the game.

Fire Pokémon have long been fan-favourites in the Pokémon franchise, which naturally extends to Pokémon TCG Pocket, with the likes of Charizard, Flareon, Entei, Moltres, and more firmly established as iconic figures.

If you’re looking to build a Fire Deck in Pokémon TCG Pocket and want to ensure you’re building the best one possible, Insider Gaming has a full build guide.

Best Fire Deck in Pokémon TCG Pocket

The best Fire-Type Decks in Pokémon TCG Pocket revolve around Mega Charizard Y, which debuted in the Crimson Blaze expansion, with Entei ex or Moltres ex standing as the best headliners to partner it with.

Entei ex has the edge over Moltres as a crucial member of the supporting card of Mega Charizard Y, with the Legendary Pulse ability allowing you to draw a card at the end of your turn if it is in the Active Spot.

More Insider Gaming: When is the Pokémon 30th Anniversary TCG Set Released?

On top of this, it has a decent attack, while the likes of Charmeleon are decent additions to the deck, as it’s Ignition ability allows you to put more Energy onto your Active Pokémon when you use it to evolve a Pokémon.

Finally, the likes of Lillie, which heals 60 damage from a Stage 2 Pokémon, Flame Patch, which adds a Fire Energy from your discard pile to your active Pokémon, and Protective Poncho, which protects a Benched Pokémon from all damage done from opponent’s attacks and Abilities, round off this strong deck.

Mega Charizard Y ex Pokémon TCG Pocket Deck Strategy Guide

Mega Charizard Y ex’s Crimson Dive attack has the ability to one-hit-KO every Pokémon in the game, though the downside is the recoil damage it takes.

Bringing Mega Charizard Y ex into the fold as quickly as possible is the priority, using the likes of Charmelon, Flame Patch, and Entei ex to accelerate the Energy onto your Active Pokémon.

More Insider Gaming: How to Get Shiny Volcanion in Pokémon Home

The likes of Professor’s Research, May, and Copycat can help to bring extra cards to your hand, if you’re missing what you need to push forward, and having Entei ex as your Active Pokémon while you gather energy onto the Charmander or Charmeleon you’re planning to eventually evolve into Mega Charizard Y ex.

Beware of Pom-Pom Oricorio, which is an effective counter to Mega Charizard Y ex, and decks centred around Magnezone and Greninja can be quicker to establish into their main strategy.

What is the main deck you’re running in Pokémon TCG Pocket? Share your deck lists in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, take a look at all the Secret Rare and Shiny cards in TCG Pocket’s Pulsing Aura, and check out all the details on the Sableye’s Gem Hunt Pokopia event.

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Producer Hints Mini-Game Arrival Is Imminent
item.content:encoded:
I’m sure you haven’t needed another reason to justify playing Resident Evil Requiem, because it is that good. Still, Capcom’s dev team has given an update on the game’s impending mini-game and what it could involve.

Resident Evil players love a mini-game, whether it involves being a ‘Mercenary’ or taking control of Tofu. For all the seriousness that Resident Evil Requiem has put you through, you should get to take the edge off soon with a new mini-game.

Resident Evil 9 Mini-Game Could Be Released Very Soon

In an interview with Japan’s Denfaminicogamer (translated), we got to hear from Requiem director Akishi Nakanishi and producer Masato Kumazawa.

One question posed to the dev duo concerned the previously announced Resident Evil 9 mini-game. There’s always some sort of mini-game content that comes after a Resi release, and Requiem will be no different.

When asked about the mini-game, Nakanishi said:

“However, as for the mini-games, it is already in the final stages of development.”

It’s confirmed that this will be a single-player mini-game, and it is “Based on the battles of the main story.”

Not only this, but Nakanishi added:

“For those who have cleared the main story and thought, ‘I haven’t gone on a rampage yet!” Wow!! I think you will enjoy it, so I hope you can sharpen your tomahawk and wait.”

However, Kumazawa was inclined to give a few more curious details away:

“This mini-game is a content that is released by clearing the main story. Therefore, if there are people who will play it in the future, I think it will be just right to play if you clear it during Golden Week.”

Golden Week is a national holiday period in Japan, occurring between April 29 and May 5.

This means the Resident Evil 9 mini-game could be dropping in the next few days!

What do you think the mini-game is? Will Capcom deviate and do something different than the usual Mercenaries mode? Or is the game mode just a given at this point? Let us know what you think it might be via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Check out the ARC Raiders Riven Tide Update 1.26.0—Complete April 28 Patch Notes for more IG news.

item.title: Last Resort Event In ARC Raiders—All Rewards & How To Earn Merits, Explained
item.content:encoded:
ARC Raiders has a myriad of different content to engage with, with the Riven Tides update adding even more to explore. With a new map and ARC threat, it’s easy to get distracted, but there’s a Live Event on the cards, and you won’t want to miss it.

Titled Last Resort, this Live Event will reward you with Merits for completing various in-game activities, which can then be used to unlock three pages of rewards, including new cosmetics and Raider Tokens.

How To Earn Merits During The Last Resort Event In ARC Raiders

You’ll earn Merits in ARC Raiders by getting XP on any map, as it’s automatically converted at the end of every raid.

100 XP converts into one Merit, so you’ll have to do a fair bit of grinding if you want to earn all of the rewards the Live Event has on offer. Thankfully, you don’t have to earn XP through specific methods, as was the case with the Shared Watch Live Event a few months back.

  • More Insider Gaming: ARC Raiders Riven Tide Update 1.26.0—Complete April 28 Patch Notes

That means you can play the game however you see fit, but if you’re desperate to get your hands on some merits fast, there’s a way you can fast-track the process—by finding Ship Models. These reward you with bonus Merits, with different rarities offering varying Merit values.

All Rewards Available During The Last Resort Event In ARC Raiders

There are 21 rewards available during the Last Resort Live Event in ARC Raiders, and you’ll need 1,050 Merits to claim them all.

  • More Insider Gaming: ARC Raiders Avian Alarm Project Guide

From Raider Tokens to exclusive cosmetics, the reward path is stacked with plenty of gifts. Every reward is listed in the table below:

When Does The Last Resort Event End In ARC Raiders?

The Last Resort Event will run for just under a month, beginning on April 28 and ending on May 25, 2026.

It’s not too long, but it should be more than enough time to earn those Merits and claim all of the available rewards. That being said, if you’re interested in those cosmetics and Raider Tokens, it’s better to begin grinding sooner rather than later.

item.title: Resident Evil's Leon Kennedy is named after a milk-guzzling hitman, Hideki Kamiya casually confirms
item.content:encoded:
Back in 1994, Luc Besson released a movie that not only introduced us to Natalie Portman in her feature film debut, but also - it turns out - inspired the name for one of gaming's most iconic characters: Leon Kennedy. That film was Léon: The Professional, and saw actor Jean Reno in the titular role of a milk-loving hitman who takes 12 year old Mathilda (played by Portman) under his wing.

Many had surmised that this film inspired Leon's name in the Resi series, but now we know for sure, as Resident Evil 2 director Hideki Kamiya recently shared a little bit of game lore on his social media.

Kamiya reflected back to when he and Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami were discussing what to call Capcom's then-Raccoon City newcomer. Here's the brief full story:

"Back when we were developing the game, I'd asked Mr Mikami for his opinion on the protagonist's name. After a few suggestions were rejected, I said, 'Right then, shall we take it from that film I saw the other day?' and proposed Leon, to which he replied, 'That's fine, I suppose'…" Kamiya shared via X (translated via DeepL), and the rest is history.

Of course, it has long been speculated that Leon Kennedy was named after Léon in the film, and there are other references to Besson's work throughout Resident Evil - the games have a pretty punishing 'Professional' difficulty setting, and there is also a handgun called Matilda.

Kamiya doesn't personally take credit for naming this weapon, though, and said it was christened Matilda by someone else at Capcom. We can probably speculate that whoever it was had made the connection between the film and Leon's name though, and decided to keep up the tradition.

As for the here and now, Leon Kennedy's latest foray into the world of mutations and chaos comes in the form of Resident Evil Requiem, a "legendary horror at an all-time high", with the character now in his early 50s but still with biceps simply desperate to burst out from his form fitting shirts.

Resident Evil Requiem has been a huge success for Capcom, becoming the fastest selling game in the series. A story DLC is currently in the works, while a new minigame is expected to release in May.

item.title: Wuchang: Fallen Feathers might have a future, with western publisher swooping in to buy rights after its dev was shut down
item.content:encoded:
Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, the popular Chinese Soulslike released in July 2025, has had its intellectual property rights fully acquired by Italian games publisher/developer Digital Bros for roughly €4m.

"Securing the IP will enhance the Group's ability to make timely decisions regarding its future development and related investments" Digital Bros announced, indicating the game may have a future after all – something that was far from certain, given the fate of its developer.

Wuchang: Fallen Feathers had a successful launch, according to industry analysis at the time, though in the following months developer Chengdu Lingzee was closed, reportedly due to the departure of key senior staff and the remaining team's rejection of a pivot to becoming a support studio.

Now, in a press release published on the Digital Bros website, the terms of the agreement have been laid out for the public. Digital Bros will now earn all revenue generated from Wuchang: Fallen Feathers, and all future decisions will be made by Digital Bros when it comes to the game's future.

The press release reads: "The Transaction secures full ownership of the IP for the Group, enabling full retention of the value generated throughout its lifecycle and eliminating any future royalty obligations to the developer. The total consideration amounts to RMB 32 million (approximately Euro 4 million), that will be funded through the Group's available credit lines."

This deal has also provided some insight into how well Wuchang: Fallen Feathers performed during its initial launch, as well as its continued popularity in the months following. According to the press release, the game was among Steam's Top Sellers list at launch, reaching over 130,000 concurrent players. It sold more than 1m copies, generating €30m, which Digital Bros says: "[confirms] the strength of the product and its long-term potential."

Digital Bros, the publisher partnered with Chengdu Lingze for the global release of Wuchang: Fallen Feathers through 505 Games, has continued to sweep up an interesting mix of properties over the years, including GhostRunner and GhostRunner 2, the Brothers: A Tale of Two Sons remake, and Indivisible.

So should fans of Wuchang: Fallen Feathers expect anything substantial soon? Probably not, as Digital Bros explained near the conclusion of the press release: "The Group does not expect the acquisition to have a significant impact on the business outlook for the current fiscal year previously disclosed to the market."

Eurogamer reviewer Kaan Serin described Wuchang: Fallen Feathers as "Soulslike comfort food", writing much of the games competency as a fun action game, but one that had "few true surprises".

Related topics
